reason: Both answers correctly land on "talk out of full event sourcing, keep an append-only ledger under CRUD," cover versioning/projection/eventual-consistency costs, correction handling, buy-vs-build (Stripe/Lago/Metronome), and question whether the team's enthusiasm is for the pattern rather than the problem. A adds a nice concrete detail (idempotent usage ingestion/dedupe) but slightly softens its verdict by conceding to scope ES into usage-rating if the team insists. B is a bit more concrete on ledger design (explicit double-entry) and ends with a firmer, clearer stance against building the tutorial project on the revenue pipeline. These differences roughly offset; neither has factual errors and both are appropriately concise and actionable.
